scope_classification.label: local_experiment
scope_classification.rationale: The topic can be explored with small simulations, synthetic data, or lightweight local experiments on the Mac.
scope_classification.feasibility_note: Offline smoke mode: feasibility uses keyword triage only.

parsed_papers.title: The AI Scientist: Towards Fully Automated Open-Ended Scientific Discovery
parsed_papers.core_claim: The paper introduces an automated pipeline capable of generating ideas, writing code, running experiments, and drafting papers for scientific discovery.
parsed_papers.possible_gap: The paper highlights the lack of robust methods for evaluating the quality and reliability of AI-generated scientific discoveries.

parsed_papers.title: The AI Scientist-v2: Workshop-Level Automated Scientific Discovery via Agentic Tree Search
parsed_papers.core_claim: The AI Scientist-v2 extends automated scientific discovery by implementing stronger search and review-like iteration to enforce stricter gates for novelty, experiment quality, and paper readiness.
limitations: Not mentioned in the provided text
parsed_papers.possible_gap: The paper's focus on internal 'stricter gates' for paper readiness leaves open the question of whether the generated work can successfully pass external, human-led peer review.

parsed_papers.title: Agent Laboratory: Using LLM Agents as Research Assistants
parsed_papers.core_claim: The paper demonstrates the use of LLM agents as research assistants for tasks like literature review, experimentation, and report writing.
parsed_papers.possible_gap: The paper identifies a gap in creating fully autonomous and reproducible research workflows, as current systems still require substantial human oversight.

claims.text: The paper introduces an automated pipeline capable of generating ideas, writing code, running experiments, and drafting papers for scientific discovery.
The AI Scientist-v2 extends automated scientific discovery by implementing stronger search and review-like iteration to enforce stricter gates for novelty, experiment quality, and paper readiness.
The paper demonstrates the use of LLM agents as research assistants for tasks like literature review, experimentation, and report writing.

links.to: The paper highlights the lack of robust methods for evaluating the quality and reliability of AI-generated scientific discoveries.
The paper's focus on internal 'stricter gates' for paper readiness leaves open the question of whether the generated work can successfully pass external, human-led peer review.
The paper identifies a gap in creating fully autonomous and reproducible research workflows, as current systems still require substantial human oversight.

research_gaps.gap_title: Traceability Mechanisms in Autonomous Research Pipelines
research_gaps.text: EV001 and EV003 introduce autonomous research agents but lack mechanisms for tracing and verifying the reasoning process, making it difficult to audit and validate agent decisions.
research_gaps.testable_hypothesis: If traceability mechanisms are implemented, ECE reduction in agent decision-making will be at least 20% compared to baseline.

research_gaps.text: EV001 and EV002 describe automated pipelines but don't optimize for sample efficiency, potentially requiring more data than necessary for effective research.
research_gaps.testable_hypothesis: If sample efficiency is optimized, the sample efficiency ratio will improve by at least 30% compared to current implementations.

research_gaps.text: EV002 mentions workshop-level work but doesn't address reproducibility, making it difficult to verify and build upon agent-generated research.
research_gaps.testable_hypothesis: If reproducibility mechanisms are implemented, the accuracy delta between original and reproduced results will be less than 5%.

research_gaps.text: EV001 and EV003 discuss agents but don't address scalability, limiting their applicability to larger, more complex research problems.
research_gaps.testable_hypothesis: If scalability improvements are implemented, the time complexity scaling factor will be reduced by at least 25% compared to baseline.

research_gaps.text: EV003 mentions LLM agents but doesn't address how they effectively integrate external knowledge, limiting their research capabilities.
research_gaps.testable_hypothesis: If external knowledge integration is improved, knowledge integration accuracy will increase by at least 15% compared to baseline.

research_gaps.text: EV001, EV002, and EV003 don't establish standardized evaluation metrics, making it difficult to compare and assess different autonomous research agents.
research_gaps.testable_hypothesis: If standardized evaluation metrics are implemented, metric consistency across different agent implementations will improve by at least 20%.

idea_tree.title: Sample Efficiency Optimization
idea_tree.pitch: EV001 and EV002 describe automated pipelines but don't optimize for sample efficiency, potentially requiring more data than necessary for effective research.
idea_tree.mechanism: If sample efficiency is optimized, the sample efficiency ratio will improve by at least 30% compared to current implementations.
idea_tree.testable_hypothesis: If sample efficiency is optimized, the sample efficiency ratio will improve by at least 30% compared to current implementations.

idea_tree.title: Scalability of Autonomous Research Agents
idea_tree.pitch: EV001 and EV003 discuss agents but don't address scalability, limiting their applicability to larger, more complex research problems.
idea_tree.mechanism: If scalability improvements are implemented, the time complexity scaling factor will be reduced by at least 25% compared to baseline.
idea_tree.testable_hypothesis: If scalability improvements are implemented, the time complexity scaling factor will be reduced by at least 25% compared to baseline.

idea_tree.title: External Knowledge Integration
idea_tree.pitch: EV003 mentions LLM agents but doesn't address how they effectively integrate external knowledge, limiting their research capabilities.
idea_tree.mechanism: If external knowledge integration is improved, knowledge integration accuracy will increase by at least 15% compared to baseline.
idea_tree.testable_hypothesis: If external knowledge integration is improved, knowledge integration accuracy will increase by at least 15% compared to baseline.
